matches.x: 2:And. David. said unto Ahimelech the priest, The king hath commanded me. 2 business, and. hath said unto me, Let no.man know any thing of the. business whereabout I- send thee, and what I have commanded. thee; and I have appointed my servants. to

A hyperbole is a statement exaggerated for effect. It is a way of verbally underscoring an idea or principle. Hyperbole or overstatement is a figure of speech common to peoples of all ages, and it is natural that the scriptures would abound with it. It can be used in flattery, as in the song of the dancing women: "Saul hath slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands" (1 Samuel 18:7).…
